perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'EFFECTIVE DATE' as '18 May 2000'. By modifying this to '15th December 2001', we create a contradiction. Clause 7.1 states that the agreement takes effect from the 'Effective Date'. So the agreement takes place on one date and comes into effect on another, therefore creating uncertainty and possible challenges around term duration and commencement of obligations. This contradicts Clause 7.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall take effect from the Effective Date and continue in full force and effect for twenty (20) years thereafter, unless otherwise terminated in accordance with Clause 8.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the 'EFFECTIVE DATE' as '18 May 2000'. The changed text modifies this to '15th December 2001'. Clause 7.1 states the agreement takes effect from the 'Effective Date' and lasts for twenty years from that date. Changing the Effective Date creates a direct contradiction because the agreement now has two different dates associated with its commencement, leading to confusion about when the 20-year term begins and potentially impacting the enforceability of obligations within that timeframe. The change directly alters a key definition that is fundamental to the operation of Clause 7.1, satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All Intellectual Property Rights and other proprietary rights in any translated, amended, revised or updated Content independently created by XFN ("AMENDED CONTENT") shall automatically vest in XFN.
perturbation.changed_text: All Intellectual Property Rights and other proprietary rights in any translated, amended, revised or updated Content independently created by XFN ("AMENDED CONTENT") shall automatically vest in CEIS.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement assigns Intellectual Property Rights of the amended content to XFN. By modifying the text to state they vest in CEIS, there is a direct conflict over who owns the rights to amended content. This affects the Grant of Rights clause, particularly clauses 2.1 and 2.2, as it becomes unclear who has the right to exploit, distribute, and sublicense the content if CEIS owns the amended content, and XFN has the license.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XFN and/or its Affiliate(s) shall have the right to charge users to access or view the Content and/or sub-license the Content to third parties for re-distribution to users. Revenues generated thereby shall be for the account of XFN or its Affiliates, and CEIS shall not be entitled to, nor make any action, claim or demand in relation thereto. XFN's only payment obligation to CEIS in consideration of the rights granted pursuant to this Clause 2 is set forth in Clause 4.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigned Intellectual Property Rights of amended content to XFN, giving XFN the right to sublicense the content and receive revenues. The changed text assigns these rights to CEIS, directly contradicting the clause that states revenues from sublicensing the content belong to XFN and that CEIS is not entitled to any of those revenues. This creates a direct conflict over who owns the right to profit from the amended content, which is a structural flaw leading to in-text contradiction. The contradiction is strong because it involves a direct transfer of ownership and associated rights, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of the revenue-sharing clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be renewed for an additional term of ten (10) years by notice in writing given by XFN to CEIS at the expiry of the Term, for a consideration to be mutually agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be renewed for an additional term of ten (10) years at CEIS's sole discretion without prior notice at the expiry of the Term, for a consideration to be determined by CEIS. If the Parties fail to resolve the dispute through negotiation, such dispute shall be referred to and be resolved by arbitration in accordance with the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules as may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that XFN can renew the agreement with written notice, and the consideration must be mutually agreed upon. By modifying the clause to say that renewal is at CEIS's sole discretion without prior notice and that the consideration will be determined by CEIS, we create a direct contradiction. Now renewal can happen, but is not necessary and there are terms to that renewal that CEIS sets, which contradicts the former text. This contradicts Clause 7.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of this Agreement through good faith consultation and negotiations. If the Parties fail to resolve the dispute through negotiation, such dispute shall be referred to and be resolved by arbitration in accordance with the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules as may be amended from time to time. The place of arbitration shall be in Hong Kong. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. There shall be one arbitrator to be agreed by Parties. If the Parties are unable to agree on an arbitrator, the International Chamber of Commerce shall appoint one.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states that disputes will be resolved through negotiation and then arbitration, with specific rules (UNCITRAL), location (Hong Kong), language (English), and arbitrator selection process outlined. The changed text introduces CEIS's sole discretion in renewing the agreement and determining consideration, directly impacting the 'Parties shall attempt to resolve any dispute' part of the contradicted text. Now, CEIS can unilaterally renew the agreement on its own terms, which contradicts the need for negotiation and arbitration as stated in the contradicted text, particularly regarding the renewal terms. This represents a strong contradiction that would lead to confusion or legal disputes.
